title: WPS Preheat Temperature Calculator
description: Determines the minimum preheat temperature for welding based on material thickness, carbon equivalent, hydrogen level, and heat input, following ISO 13916 and AWS D1.1 standards.

inputs.label: Material Thickness (mm)
inputs.businessContext: Thickness of the base metal to be welded, measured in millimeters.

inputs.label: Carbon Equivalent (CE)
inputs.businessContext: Carbon equivalent value calculated from material composition (IIW formula).

inputs.label: Hydrogen Level (ml/100g weld metal)
inputs.businessContext: Diffusible hydrogen content of the welding consumable, per ISO 3690.

inputs.label: Heat Input (kJ/mm)
inputs.businessContext: Arc energy per unit length of weld, calculated from voltage, current, and travel speed.

inputs.label: Joint Restraint Factor
inputs.businessContext: Degree of mechanical restraint on the weld joint, affecting crack susceptibility.

inputs.label: Preheat Method
inputs.businessContext: Method used to apply preheat, influences heating rate and uniformity.

rules.message: For thickness > 50 mm and CE > 0.5, preheat must be at least 150°C per ISO 13916.
High hydrogen with low heat input requires minimum preheat of 200°C.

thresholds.message: Calculated preheat is below 50°C; consider minimum 50°C for moisture removal.
Calculated preheat exceeds 300°C; verify inputs or consider alternative welding procedure.

formulas.expression: if carbon_equivalent <= 0.35 then 0 else if carbon_equivalent <= 0.55 then (carbon_equivalent - 0.35) * 200 else (carbon_equivalent - 0.35) * 250
case hydrogen_level when 'low' then 0 when 'medium' then 30 when 'high' then 70

formulas.description: Adjustment for heat input (lower heat input requires higher preheat)
formulas.expression: if heat_input < 1.0 then (1.0 - heat_input) * 80 else if heat_input <= 2.5 then 0 else (heat_input - 2.5) * -20
case joint_restraint when 'low' then 0 when 'moderate' then 20 when 'high' then 50

formulas.description: Raw preheat temperature before method adjustment
Final recommended preheat temperature rounded to nearest 5°C, with method efficiency correction
formulas.expression: round(preheat_temperature_raw * (case preheat_method when 'electric' then 1.0 when 'gas' then 1.1 when 'induction' then 0.95) / 5) * 5

primary.label: Recommended Preheat Temperature
primary.description: Minimum preheat temperature to be applied before welding.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Moisture Risk
hiddenLossDrivers.description: If preheat < 50°C, moisture may not be fully removed, increasing hydrogen cracking risk.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Thermal Stress
hiddenLossDrivers.description: High preheat (>250°C) may cause excessive thermal stress and distortion in thin sections.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Energy Cost Impact
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Higher preheat temperatures increase energy consumption and cycle time.

suggestedActions.label: Verify Carbon Equivalent
suggestedActions.description: Confirm actual material composition and recalculate CE using IIW formula.

suggestedActions.label: Check Consumable Hydrogen
suggestedActions.description: Use low-hydrogen electrodes or flux to reduce preheat requirement.

suggestedActions.label: Optimize Heat Input
suggestedActions.description: Increase travel speed or voltage to raise heat input and lower needed preheat.

suggestedActions.label: Monitor Preheat Uniformity
suggestedActions.description: Use temperature-indicating crayons or infrared sensors to ensure uniform heating.
